overview: Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM-JANMAN) is a mission launched by the Hon'ble Prime Minister on November 15, 2023, to uplift Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) by providing targeted support in safe housing, clean drinking water, education, health, road and telecom connectivity, electrification, and sustainable livelihoods. The mission aims to improve the quality of life for PVTG communities through 11 critical interventions implemented by 9 line Ministries, with the Ministry of Tribal Affairs as the nodal agency. It focuses on saturating PVTG households and habitations with basic facilities in a time-bound manner during the mission period (2023-24 to 2025-26).
geographic_scope: 18 States and 1 Union Territory (Andaman & Nicobar Islands)
objectives: Improve socio-economic conditions of PVTGs by saturating households and habitations with basic facilities
Provide access to secure and habitable housing for every PVTG household
Guarantee access to clean and safe drinking water sources and proper sanitation facilities
Enhance accessibility to quality education with focus on increasing enrollment and retention among PVTG children
Ensure easy access to healthcare services, regular check-ups, and nutrition programs to improve health outcomes
Establish road infrastructure, electricity, and telecom connectivity to facilitate economic opportunities and access to information
Promote sustainable livelihood options within or near PVTG habitations to reduce dependency on marginal resources
Provide vocational education and skilling opportunities for economic empowerment
Improve health and nutrition status through Anganwadi worker, ANM, and ASHA worker interventions
Streamline access to government services via centralized single-window service centres (Multi-Purpose Centres)
Facilitate informed decision-making through services like VDVK centres, skill centres, and inputs on social, economic, and health issues
eligibility: The mission targets 75 Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) residing in 18 States and a Union Territory (Andaman and Nicobar Islands) who were unreached by existing schemes and require multi-sectoral handholding. Eligible habitations are those with 50-100 PVTG population lacking VDVK centres and Skill Centres, with priority given to habitations without Anganwadi Centre and Health Sub-Centres, then those without Health Sub-Centre, and finally those without Anganwadi Centres. States must update habitation-wise survey data on the PM Gati Shakti Portal and conduct stakeholder consultations at Gram Sabha/Panchayat level for land, location, design, and construction oversight.
benefits: Financial grant up to Rs. 60 lakh per Multi-Purpose Centre (MPC) for construction; provision of services including nutrition (Anganwadi worker), health (ANM, ASHA), skill & vocational education (Rural Self Employment Training Institutes), livelihood generation (VDVKs), financial inclusion (Corporate BC-NRLM), and community cultural programmes (Gram Panchayat/Traditional leaders); recurring costs for workers in Anganwadi, ANM & ASHA services and drug availability provided by respective line Ministries/Departments and State Governments; funds released in two instalments (50% first, balance after Statement of Expenditure and Utilisation Certificate); land requirement of approximately half acre for MPC construction (2200 sq ft built-up area); technical and administrative support through Tribal Welfare Department coordination with State Women and Child Development and Health departments; monitoring via District Level Coordination Committee (DLCC) and State Level Coordination Committee (SLCC); data updation on PM Gati Shakti Portal for physical and financial progress.
financial_support: 100% grant (up to Rs. 60 lakh per MPC for construction only) provided by Ministry of Tribal Affairs under PM-JANMAN; funds transferred to State nodal department/Tribal Welfare Department, which authorizes funds to District Collector/District Magistrate or implementing agency as decided by the State Government; financial assistance released in two instalments: first instalment of 50% and final instalment after receiving Statement of Expenditure (SoE) and Utilisation Certificate (UC) from the State and utilization of funds as per DOE guidelines for SNA accounts; recurring costs for services (Anganwadi, ANM, ASHA workers, drug availability) borne by respective line Ministries/Departments and State Governments as per PM-JANMAN guidelines.
application_process: 1. State Government identifies eligible PVTG habitations/villages based on criteria (50-100 PVTG population, lacking VDVK and Skill Centres) and updates habitation-wise survey data on PM Gati Shakti Portal. 2. State conducts stakeholder consultation at Gram Sabha/Panchayat level for land, location, design, and construction oversight. 3. State ensures land availability (approximately half acre) and transfers ownership to State Tribal Welfare Department (TWD). 4. State prepares Detailed Project Report (DPR) with cost estimates approved by competent authority and agency executing the project. 5. State submits proposal through Principal Secretary/Secretary, Tribal Welfare Department for approval by Project Approval Committee (PAC) at Ministry of Tribal Affairs. 6. Proposal examined by PM-JANMAN-associated division before sending for approval by Committee chaired by Mission Director/JS of PM-JANMAN. 7. Approval communicated after financial concurrence of Internal Finance Division (IFD). 8. Funds released in two instalments: 50% first instalment, balance after SoE and UC submission. 9. State updates physical and financial progress on PM Gati Shakti Portal via dedicated module. 10. State TWD submits detailed report to MoTA after complying with stipulations (land ownership, construction on Govt/Public land, DLCC recommendation, Principal Secretary approval, transparent execution by Govt. agency, cost not exceeding Rs. 60 lakh, technical scrutiny by competent authority, clearances obtained).
required_documents: Location of the MPC with approval of Gram Sabha if required
Name of the Agency identified by State/District for construction
State/District has entered in MOU with the identified Agency
Registration of District Magistrate/District Collectors/Agency on PFMS portal
Approval of Proposal by committee chaired by District Magistrate/District Collectors
caveats: Construction must be carried out only on Government/Public land
Construction cost including furniture, fixture, fittings, and other basic requirements must not exceed Rs. 60 lakh
State must ensure land identified is adequate for construction and ownership transferred to State TWD
Recommendation of DLCC and approval of Principal Secretary on the proposal must be obtained
Technical scrutiny of estimates must be done by competent authority as per DFPR applicable in the state
Project must be executed by Govt. agency in a transparent manner
All necessary clearances under Central or State Act/Rules/Regulations must be obtained by the State
State TWD must submit detailed report to MoTA after complying with stipulations
Financial and physical progress of MPC must be updated on PM Gati Shakti Portal

Wherever there is Anganwadi or Mini Anganwadi with staff and Anganwadi is kachha, the same will be shifted to the MPC; if Pucca Anganwadi with staff exists in same habitation/village, no separate staff for Anganwadi will be provided in MPC
State must finalise mechanism for maintenance of MPCs as per guidelines and coordinate with concerned line departments for provision of staff
Guidelines may be amended or relaxed with approval of Hon’ble Minister of Tribal Affairs depending on administrative requirement
